name: MCP News Feed
description: News about MCP, AI agents, MCP servers, tool integrations, security, developer tooling, and enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: We taught AI agents to check who they're talking to (build notes)
articles.summary: My coding agent will connect to anything. Yours will too. Point Claude Code, Cursor or Codex at an MCP server and it connects, lists the tools, and starts calling them. The server describes itself, and the agent believes it. "A safe and convenient way to manage your repositories" is not a fact...

articles.title: MCP in 2026: The numbers behind the ecosystem explosion
articles.summary: I spent an afternoon digging through the MCP ecosystem numbers. Here is what I found. articles.title: We stopped giving AI agents database passwords
articles.summary: Every "connect your AI agent to a database" tutorial ends the same way: paste a connection string into a config file. That string now lives on a laptop, in shell history, maybe in a Slack DM to unblock someone on a Friday. We didn't want that for our own team, so we built db-mcp-gateway a...

articles.title: Bothread: A Free, Local Room Where Your AI Coding Agents Stop Overwriting Each Other
articles.summary: If you've run more than one AI coding agent on the same project, you already know the failure mode. You point Claude Code at /src/game and Cursor at /src/ui "just to be safe," and twenty minutes later one of them has quietly rewritten a file the other was mid-edit on. No error, no warning — just a...

articles.title: I spent a week on OAuth plumbing for an MCP server before writing a single line of actual product logic.
articles.summary: I spent a week on OAuth plumbing for an MCP server before writing a single line of actual product logic. Four hours in I was reading RFC 9728. articles.title: Hong Kong Companies Registry: live filings without cache lag
articles.summary: Hong Kong company numbers survive renames. During IPO diligence, that identifier is often more dependable than the trading-name history copied across bank files and legacy paid databases. The Hong Kong Companies Registry (公司註冊處) exposes enough structured data to trace entities across borders...

articles.title: DevSpace: a coding agent that keeps your code on your own machine
articles.summary: A coding agent that never uploads your code DevSpace inverts the usual bargain of AI coding assistants. articles.title: Your CLAUDE.md is not your agent's memory
articles.summary: I like CLAUDE.md. I also think people are asking it to do a job it cannot do. CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and .cursor/rules are where you put the rules you want an agent to follow: how to run tests, which folders are sensitive, what style the team uses, and when to ask before deploying. That is written...

articles.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
articles.summary: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. articles.author: Brooke Mosby

articles.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
articles.summary: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
articles.author: Will Papper

articles.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
articles.summary: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.

articles.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced

articles.author: vzisiadis

articles.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
articles.summary: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
